title: Complete the area listener management surface and land real collector-side publish before returning to onboarding runtime blockers
releaseGate: Owner-directed sequencing change: do not prioritize the managed-listener runtime/worker blocker next. First make the area listener page functionally complete from its highlighted create entries through real collector-side config publish/apply, then decide whether a later onboarding/runtime repair round is still needed.

stories.title: Complete the area listener quick-create and CRUD workflow
stories.scope: Make the highlighted quick-create entries on `#/platform/area-listener-services` lead to a complete usable workflow instead of a wrapper-only shell: each header/card entry must open the correct preset, save a valid listener plan, refresh into the table, and allow edit plus release-modal entry from the resulting row.
nonGoals: Do not claim runtime publish success in this story.
Do not widen into Device V2 row-action onboarding reruns.
Do not redesign the page away from the existing platform visual language.
acceptance: The three header create buttons and the three card `新增` entries open the correct listener preset and are no longer dead-end shells.
A saved listener row appears in the table with usable edit and release-modal entrypoints.
Typecheck still passes after the workflow completion changes.
notes: Must read first: the owner-marked screenshot feedback, `workstreams/07-independent-listener-management-page.md`, and `OneOPS-UI/src/views/platform/AreaListenerServiceManagement.vue`.
Treat the issue as functional completeness, not merely click wiring.

stories.title: Land real server and network syslog listener publish execution
stories.scope: Finish the platform-side publish path for `server_syslog_listener` and `network_syslog_listener` so the area listener page can perform concrete collector-side `dry-run / preflight / apply / remove` work and return exact deployment evidence instead of stopping at wrapper semantics or ambiguous placeholder-only results.
nonGoals: Do not implement device-side syslog target remote configuration in this story.
Do not widen into SNMP trap publish in this story.
Do not paraphrase missing collector-side behavior as success.
acceptance: For server/network syslog listener services, release actions now surface concrete collector-side publish results and updated status fields.
The page no longer relies on wrapper-only semantics to describe a finished syslog listener publish path.
notes: Use the existing `remote_syslog / log_forward_plan` chain only when it can produce real collector-side deployment truth.
If a remaining backend adapter contract blocks real publish, record it exactly.

stories.title: Land real SNMP Trap listener publish support on the area listener page
stories.scope: Replace the current `snmp_trap_listener` adapter-gap-only behavior with a real collector-side trap-listener publish path on the area listener page. Keep the boundary explicit: this story owns area listener publish only and still does not include device-side trap target remote configuration.
nonGoals: Do not implement device-side SNMP trap target delivery.
Do not widen into unrelated telemetry or onboarding work.
Do not keep the page-level SNMP Trap entry as a permanent half-finished shell.
acceptance: The SNMP Trap create/publish flow no longer closes only with the old adapter-gap placeholder.
Release actions now return exact collector-side trap listener publish truth or a narrower exact blocker.
notes: Honor the product boundary: make the page-level SNMP Trap listener entry real, but stop short of device-side trap target delivery.
Do not treat the existing `adapter_gap` honesty as sufficient completion anymore.

stories.title: Run one live platform-side listener verification after functional completion
stories.scope: Run one bounded live verification pass from the independent area listener management surface after the functional-completion stories land. Capture the exact create/save/release evidence for the repaired listener flows and state explicitly whether the program should next reopen the managed-listener runtime/onboarding thread or whether another platform-side repair round is still required.
nonGoals: Do not return to Device V2 onboarding reruns in this story.
Do not create a docs-only closeout without live listener evidence.
Do not widen into batch listener provisioning.
acceptance: The evidence records exact create/save/release results for the newly completed listener flows.
The next-step note explicitly says whether a later batch should return to onboarding managed-listener runtime/blocker repair.
Focused controller-owned validation gates are left runnable.
notes: This story closes the owner-directed functional-completion round.
If the live page still exposes a narrower exact blocker, preserve it without jumping back to worker/runtime repair in the same story.
